title: When subitem is done, move subitem to done
excerpt: I want to move a done subitem to the done group on my board. In automations, it looks like I can only move the entire item, not just individual subitems. Thanks for your help.

posts.author: pcarlaw (Peggy Carlaw)
posts.cookedHtml: I want to move a done subitem to the done group on my board. In automations, it looks like I can only move the entire item, not just individual subitems. Thanks for your help.

posts.author: lermag (Gilad Lerman)
posts.cookedHtml:
This is very much needed!
When I’ve change a subitem status to Done I would like it to move to the “Done” group. This is what I do with items so that I can see they now have a “Done” color in the Timeline. Is it possible to do the same with done subitems and move them to the “Done” group so that the will have different color on the timeline?
Thanks a lot for the help.

posts.author: NHS_User1 (Nazneen Bagdadi)
posts.cookedHtml:
Absolutely agree with this. Sometimes actions have to be categorised and actions within added as subitems. Unfortunatly this means there a tonne of actions completed but still remain within the category. It would be really helpful if there was an automation that stated:
if subitem = completed, then move to Closed group.
thanks

posts.author: Soroh (Soroh Donn)
posts.cookedHtml: Would you want the item to come up twice - once with the done subitem, and once with the other subitems? Do you want the subitem to move on its own without the item to indicate what the subitem ‘belongs’ to? Sounds great and I would also want such a thing, I just can’t figure out the best way to do this.

posts.author: NHS_User1 (Nazneen Bagdadi)
posts.cookedHtml: not to come up twice. just want it moved to the completed group. If needs be select the data items that will not copy across as its going over as an item rather than sub item, and thats fine - it does this anyway if the columns don’t match.

posts.author: Soroh (Soroh Donn)
posts.cookedHtml: But what’s if one subitem is marked as done, and another is not? Where should it go?

posts.author: NHS_User1 (Nazneen Bagdadi)
posts.cookedHtml: if subitem is done, it goes to completed. if others are still in progress then they remain where they are.

posts.author: Fantasy-Media-Team (Fantasy Media Team)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @NHS_User1, @Soroh, @lermag, @pcarlaw

We developed an app called subitem automations that provides robust automation capabilities to subitems. It also includes the ability to move subitems between boards (and more) here’s an example →

Move between boards →

Hope that helps!
